mcqs.question: Which scheduling criterion ensures that the CPU remains busy as much as possible?
mcqs.explanation: CPU Utilization measures the percentage of time the CPU is actively executing a thread. Maximizing CPU utilization ensures efficient use of expensive CPU resources.

mcqs.question: In Round Robin scheduling, what happens when the time quantum expires?

options.text: Thread moves to back of ready queue

mcqs.explanation: When a thread uses its allocated time quantum, it is preempted and placed at the back of the ready queue, allowing other threads to execute.

mcqs.question: Which thread model provides true parallelism on multiprocessor systems?
mcqs.explanation: One-to-one model maps each user thread to a kernel thread, allowing the OS to schedule different threads on different CPU cores simultaneously.

mcqs.question: What is a major disadvantage of user-level threads?

options.text: If one thread blocks, all threads in process block

mcqs.explanation: User-level threads rely on kernel-level process scheduling; if any thread makes blocking system call, the entire process (all its threads) blocks because kernel sees only one thread.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm minimizes average waiting time (when burst times are known)?
mcqs.explanation: Shortest Job First (SJF) always selects the shortest burst time, which mathematically proven to provide minimum average waiting time among all scheduling algorithms.

mcqs.question: What is the main problem with SJF scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Long-running threads may never get CPU time if short threads keep arriving, causing starvation. This is called the starvation or aging problem.

mcqs.question: In which scheduling type does the thread keep CPU until it voluntarily yields or blocks?
mcqs.explanation: Non-preemptive scheduling allows a thread to run until it completes or voluntarily gives up CPU. This prevents context switch overhead but can cause poor response time.

mcqs.question: Thread affinity refers to:

options.text: Binding thread to specific CPU core

mcqs.explanation: Thread affinity binds a thread to particular CPU core(s), improving cache performance by keeping thread data in local cache.

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a thread scheduling level?
mcqs.explanation: Long-term, short-term, and medium-term are process scheduling levels. Thread scheduling primarily uses short-term (CPU) scheduling. Real-time is a scheduling class, not a level.

mcqs.question: Which metric measures time from request submission to first response?
mcqs.explanation: Response time is the interval between thread submission and when it starts executing. Crucial for interactive systems where user sees quick feedback.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of kernel-level threads over user-level threads?
mcqs.explanation: Kernel-level threads are visible to OS scheduler, so multiple threads can run simultaneously on different processors in multiprocessor systems.

mcqs.question: In a multiprocessor system, which scheduling approach treats all CPUs equally?
mcqs.explanation: Symmetric multiprocessing (SMP) treats all CPUs equally, with a common ready queue or per-CPU queues, allowing any thread to run on any CPU.

mcqs.question: What happens during a thread context switch?

options.text: Only CPU registers and program counter are saved/restored

mcqs.explanation: Thread context switch saves/restores CPU registers, program counter, stack pointer, and thread-local storage. It is faster than process switch since address space remains same.

mcqs.question: Round Robin is best suited for:
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin provides fair time allocation and good response time, making it ideal for time-sharing systems where multiple users share the CPU.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm gives priority to older processes (to prevent starvation)?
mcqs.explanation: Aging gradually increases priority of waiting processes; FCFS with aging means longer-waiting threads get higher priority, preventing starvation.

mcqs.question: What is the primary benefit of many-to-many threading model?

options.text: Combines parallelism with flexibility of user threads

mcqs.explanation: Many-to-many maps multiple user threads to fewer kernel threads, providing parallelism while allowing blocking calls without blocking entire process.

mcqs.question: Throughput in thread scheduling refers to:

options.text: Number of threads completed per time unit

mcqs.explanation: Throughput measures system efficiency - the number of threads completed per unit time. Higher throughput means better system utilization.

mcqs.question: Which of the following increases thread scheduling overhead?
mcqs.explanation: More frequent context switches (smaller quantum, more preemptions) increase overhead because CPU time is spent saving/restoring state rather than doing useful work.

mcqs.question: In priority scheduling, what is the issue with fixed priorities?
mcqs.explanation: Fixed priority scheduling can cause starvation - low priority threads may never get CPU if high priority threads keep arriving.

mcqs.question: What is the turnaround time of a thread?

options.text: Time from thread creation to completion
Time from submission to first response

mcqs.explanation: Turnaround time = Completion time - Arrival time. It includes waiting time, execution time, and all other delays.
